title: Configuration Management — Drift on CIS Hardening Parameters for Web Tier

spl:
index=config sourcetype=inspec:report control_profile=web_cis earliest=-7d
| where status="failed"
| stats count as failed_controls by host, control_id
| where failed_controls>0
description: Compares daily config snapshots to golden baseline for web servers in scope.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes config and sourcetypes inspec:report. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Version baselines; (2) emergency change exceptions; (3) quarterly config review. Indexes required: `index=config`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=inspec:report`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for compares daily config snapshots to golden baseline for web servers in scope. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Chef Inspec or Ansible HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `inspec:report`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Version baselines; (2) emergency change exceptions; (3) quarterly config review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=config sourcetype=inspec:report control_profile=web_cis earliest=-7d
| where status="failed"
| stats count as failed_controls by host, control_id
| where failed_controls>0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Configuration Management — Drift on CIS Hardening Parameters for Web Tier (A.8.9)** — Compares daily config snapshots to golden baseline for web servers in scope.

Documented **Data sources**: `inspec:report`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Chef Inspec or Ansible HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: config; **sourcetype**: inspec:report. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=config, sourcetype=inspec:report, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where status="failed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, control_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where failed_controls>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Configuration Management — Drift on CIS Hardening Parameters for Web Tier (A.8.9)** — Compares daily config snapshots to golden baseline for web servers in scope.

Documented **Data sources**: `inspec:report`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Chef Inspec or Ansible HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap, Table
